chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Intent-Verified Development: The Book
# ==============================================================================
# System-level intent for creating a top-selling book on IVD that teaches
# principles, validates them through real projects, and proves effectiveness.
#
# Version: 1.3
# Created: 2026-01-25
# Status: Planning
# Reference: ../ivd_system_intent.yaml (IVD Framework System Intent)
# ==============================================================================

scope:
  level: "system"
  type: "product"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this book accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "The definitive book on Intent-Verified Development (IVD): the framework for the AI Agents era that eliminates many-turns and hallucinations by having AI write, implement, and verify intent."
  
  goal: |
    Produce a comprehensive book that fully explains Intent-Verified Development for the AI Agents era:
    
    Core Problem Solved:
    - AI agents fill gaps in prompts with plausible but wrong assumptions (hallucinations)
    - Working with AI requires many turns of correction (exhausting, expensive)
    - Traditional artifacts (PRDs, user stories, prompts) are not verifiable by AI
    
    IVD Solution:
    - The AI writes a structured intent artifact (not the human)
    - The AI implements against the intent artifact
    - The AI verifies its code against executable constraints
    - Turns drop from many to one; hallucinations are caught at the source
    
    Book Structure:
    1. Part I: Paradigm and principles (AI writes intent, AI verifies)
    2. Part II: Practice (5 real projects with AI agents)
    3. Part III: Validation (measurable results)
    4. Part IV: Adoption (organizational playbook)
    
    A reader who finishes this book understands IVD completely and can immediately work with AI agents using intent-verified workflows.
  
  success_metric: |
    Market Performance:
    - #1 ranking in "AI & Machine Learning" category on Amazon
    - 1,000+ reviews with 4.5+ star average
    - 10,000+ copies sold in first year
    - Featured in AI/tech publications and podcasts
    
    Technical Quality:
    - All principles validated with real project data
    - 10+ case studies with measurable outcomes
    - Executable code examples and templates
    - 95%+ reader satisfaction on "immediate applicability"
    
    Impact:
    - 1,000+ teams adopt IVD from the book
    - Community contributions to IVD framework
    - Industry recognition and speaking engagements
  
  stakeholders:
    - "Developers and engineers working with AI agents (Cursor, Copilot, Claude, GPT)"
    - "Technical leads managing teams that use AI for implementation"
    - "Indie developers and startups who rely on AI to build fast"
    - "Anyone tired of many turns and hallucinations when working with AI"
    - "Computer science educators teaching AI-assisted development"

# =================================================

satisfaction on "immediate applicability"
    
    Impact:
    - 1,000+ teams adopt IVD from the book
    - Community contributions to IVD framework
    - Industry recognition and speaking engagements
  
  stakeholders:
    - "Developers and engineers working with AI agents (Cursor, Copilot, Claude, GPT)"
    - "Technical leads managing teams that use AI for implementation"
    - "Indie developers and startups who rely on AI to build fast"
    - "Anyone tired of many turns and hallucinations when working with AI"
    - "Computer science educators teaching AI-assisted development"

# ==============================================================================
# BOOK STRUCTURE: Journey from Problem to Solution to Proof
# ==============================================================================

book_structure:
  
  front_matter:
    - section: "Cover & Title Page"
      status: "pending"
      placeholder: |
        Title: "Intent-Verified Development: The Framework for the AI Agents Era"
        Subtitle: "Stop the Many-Turns. End the Hallucinations. Let AI Write, Build, and Verify."
        Author: Leo Celis
    
    - section: "Table of Contents"
      status: "pending"
      placeholder: |
        Detailed, multi-level TOC to support reference and teaching (pattern: Code Complete, CLRS, Design Patterns).
        Auto-generated from chapter structure; readers should find sections by scan.
    
    - section: "Foreword"
      status: "pending"
      placeholder: |
        By [Industry Leader - TBD]
        - The AI revolution's missing piece
        - Why intent matters more than code
        - Personal experience with IVD adoption
    
    - section: "Preface: Why This Book Exists"
      status: "pending"
      placeholder: |
        - Establish the problem and who this is for in the first 2 pages (pattern: Code Complete, Clean Code).
        - The $10B problem: misalignment in human-AI collaboration
        - My journey: discovering intent as the primary artifact
        - What you'll learn, build, and validate
        - How this book is different (evidence-based, tested, proven)
        - Who should read this book
    
    - section: "How to Use This Book"
      status: "pending"
      placeholder: |
        Three reading paths (each delivers value at exit; pattern: Code Complete):
        1. Practitioner Path: Part I → Part II. Focus on doing; skip or skim Part III–IV. Exit: building with IVD.
        2. Leader Path: Part I → Part III → Part IV. Understand ROI, proof, and adoption. Exit: making the case and scaling.
        3. Complete Path: Read sequentially. Full mastery and reference.
        Code repository, templates, and appendices (Quick Reference, Recipe Library) support dip-in use.
        Community and support resources.

  # ==========================================================================
  # PART I: THE PARADIGM SHIFT
  # ==========================================================================
  
  part_1_foundations:
    title: "Part I: The Paradigm Shift - Why Intent is Primary"
    purpose: "Establish the problem and the IVD paradigm; reader understands why intent is primary (theory-first; pattern: SICP,

and scaling.

        Code repository, templates, and appendices (Quick Reference, Recipe Library) support dip-in use.

  # ==========================================================================
  # PART I: THE PARADIGM SHIFT

  part_1_foundations:
    title: "Part I: The Paradigm Shift - Why Intent is Primary"
    purpose: "Establish the problem and the IVD paradigm; reader understands why intent is primary (theory-first; pattern: SICP, Code Complete)."
    chapters:
      
      - chapter: 1
        title: "The Alignment Crisis"
        status: "pending"
        key_points:
          - "Traditional development: code is primary, docs are secondary (and wrong)"
          - "Human-AI collaboration fragility: multiple iterations, compounding misalignment"
          - "The $10B problem: 85-90% knowledge loss in software projects"
          - "Real examples: projects that failed due to misalignment"
        placeholder: |
          1.1 The Expensive Pattern: "Build X" → "Here's X" → "No, I meant..."
          1.2 Why Traditional Documentation Fails
          1.3 The AI Amplification Effect: Misalignment at Machine Speed
          1.4 The Core Question: What If Intent Was Verifiable?
      
      - chapter: 2
        title: "Intent-Verified Development: A New Paradigm"
        status: "complete"
        key_points:
          - "The fundamental shift: Intent → Code → Docs (not Code → Docs)"
          - "Intent as a verifiable contract between human and AI"
          - "The eight principles of IVD"
          - "Why this works: executable understanding fails loudly"
        placeholder: |
          2.1 The Three Paradigms: Traditional → Literate → Intent-Verified
          2.2 The Core Insight: Intent is Primary
          2.3 The Contract Model: A Shared Artifact Both Parties Verify
          2.4 IVD in Action: The PM/Dev CSV Example Revisited
          2.5 The Eight Principles: An Overview
          2.6 Why This Works: Executable Understanding Fails Loudly
      
      - chapter: 3
        title: "The Eight Principles in Depth"
        status: "pending"

          - "How principles work together as a system"
          - "Common violations and how to avoid them"
        placeholder: |
          3.1 Principle 1: Intent is Primary (one-line rule first, then 1–2 paragraphs)
          3.2 Principle 2: Understanding Must Be Executable
          3.3 Principle 3: Bidirectional Synchronization
          3.4 Principle 4: Continuous Verification

          3.6 Principle 6: AI as Understanding Partner
          3.7 Principle 7: Understanding Survives Implementation
          3.8 Principle 8: Innovation through Inversion (when to use, 4 steps, inversion_opportunities)
          3.9 The System View: How Principles Reinforce Each Other

        status: "pending"

        placeholder: |
          4.1 Anatomy of an Intent Artifact

          4.4 From Intent to Implementation (the flow)
          4.5 Common Mistakes and How to Fix Them
          4.6 Template Library and Quick Reference

  part_4_adoption:
    title: "Part IV: Adoption - Making IVD Work for Your Team"
    purpose: "Adopt and scale IVD in teams and organizations; from first week to ecosystem."
    chapters:
      
      - chapter: 17
        title: "Getting Started: The First Week"
        status: "pending"
        key_points:
          - "Two paths: greenfield (new projects) vs brownfield (existing projects)"
          - "Brownfield: Initialize IVD with project context (ivd init)"
          - "Project context: code rules, architecture, tools, 'map to the stars' (key_paths)"
          - "Child intents reference parent_intent (system intent)"

        placeholder: |
          17.1 Two Starting Points: Greenfield vs Brownfield
              - Greenfield: Start with first module, conventions emerge
              - Brownfield: Initialize with ivd init, capture existing context
          
          17.2 Brownfield: Day 1 - Bootstrap IVD with Project Context
              - Run ivd init to scan project
              - Creates system_intent.yaml with:
                * code_rules (.cursorrules, lint configs)
                * architecture (ARCHITECTURE.md, ADRs, principles)
                * tools_scripts (key scripts, CLIs)
                * libraries_reuse (internal shared libs)
                * key_paths ("map to the stars": entrypoints, modules, workflows, docs, tests)
                * existing_docs (README, API.md, CONTRIBUTING)
              - Review and enrich auto-generated context
          
          17.3 Day 2-3: First Intent Artifacts for Critical Modules
              - Identify 3-5 critical modules (not entire codebase)
              - Use ivd scaffold --level module --name <module>
              - Auto-links parent_intent: "../../system_intent.yaml"
              - AI loads project context when creating/modifying intents
          
          17.4 Day 4-5: Implement and Verify with Project Context
              - New intents follow project code rules
              - New intents reference architecture principles
              - New intents reuse tools/scripts/libraries (no duplication)
              - New intents navigate via key_paths (map to the stars)
          
          17.5 Week 1 Retrospective: What Worked, What Didn't
              - Did project_context capture conventions correctly?
              - Are child intents referencing parent correc

# ==============================================================================
# WRITING STYLE (from research/best_selling_programming_books.md)
# ==============================================================================
# Patterns from Code Complete, Clean Code, Pragmatic Programmer, Design Patterns.
# ==============================================================================

writing_style:
  voice:
    primary: "mentor-like, direct"
    person: "second person ('you')"
    mood: "imperative, actionable; tell the reader what to do"
    examples: "Care about your craft (Pragmatic). The only way to go fast is to go well (Clean Code)."
  
  tone:
    target: "high depth, high accessibility (Code Complete, Clean Code pattern)"
    balance: "Dense where needed, but always structured with checklists and summaries so it stays scannable and applicable."
    avoid: "Purely academic (SICP/CLRS) or purely informal; we are expert and practical."
  
  code_and_prose:
    par

==========================================

writing_style:
  voice:
    primary: "mentor-like, direct"
    person: "second person ('you')"
    mood: "imperative, actionable; tell the reader what to do"
    examples: "Care about your craft (Pragmatic). The only way to go fast is to go well (Clean Code)."
  
  tone:
    target: "high depth, high accessibility (Code Complete, Clean Code pattern)"
    balance: "Dense where needed, but always structured with checklists and summaries so it stays scannable and applicable."
    avoid: "Purely academic (SICP/CLRS) or purely informal; we are expert and practical."
  
  code_and_prose:
    part_1_3_4: "Prose leads; code and intent-artifact examples illustrate. Prose explains and generalizes."
    part_2: "Code and intent artifacts are centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale. 'Build this' drives learning (AtBS, Head First pattern)."
  
  clarity_tactics:
    - "Analogies and metaphors: e.g. 'Intent as contract,' 'executable understanding,' 'alignment not hope,' 'from hope it works to prove it works'"
    - "Diagrams: flow, structure, intent-to-code relationship (Design Patterns, Code Complete pattern)"
    - "Chapter and section summaries: 'Key Points' or 'Summary' at chapter end (Code Complete)"
    - "Checklists: verification, adoption, pre-flight (Code Complete); use in chapters and Appendix A"
    - "Rules and lists: short rules for principles; IVD's 8 principles as memorable, tweetable one-liners"
    - "Exercises and projects: Part II projects; optional end-of-chapter exercises where they add value"
  
  analogies_and_metaphors:
    canonical:
      - "Intent as a verifiable contract between human and AI"
      - "Executable understanding fails loudly"
      - "Alignment, not hope"
      - "From 'Hope It Works' to 'Prove It Works'"
      - "Intent is primary; code and docs follow"
    use: "Weave these into preface, Part I, and Part III; reuse where they reinforce."
  
  quotable_lines:
    requirement: "Include 1–2 memorable, tweetable lines per chapter where natural (Pragmatic, Clean Code pattern)."
    examples: "Intent is primary. Understanding must be executable. Verify, don't hope."
  
  principles_as_rules:
    requirement: "The 7 IVD principles must be stateable as short, memorable rules (Clean Code: 'Do one thing,' 'Stepdown')."
    format: "One-line rule + 1–2 paragraphs explanation; repeat the rule in chapter summaries and Appendix A."
  
  formatting_conventions:
    emphasis:
      bold: "Use for KEY TERMS on first introduction (e.g., **Intent-Verified Development**), emphasis in Key Points bullets (e.g., **Traditional development: code is primary**), and quotable lines (e.g., **We have been building at the speed of misunderstanding.**)"
      italic: "Use for emphasis within sentences (e.g., '*code is primary*'), book/article titles (e.g., *Code Complete*), technical terms in prose context (e.g., 'the *intent* artifact'), and quoted speech/dialogue (e.g., '*Build X.*')"
      code: "Use for code snippets (`variable_name`), file paths (`intent.yaml`), commands (`ivd validate`), and technical identifiers"
    
    quotations:
      inline_quotes: "Use double quotes for short phr

ed Development**), emphasis in Key Points bullets (e.g., **Traditional development: code is primary**), and quotable lines (e.g., **We have been building at the speed of misunderstanding.**)"
      italic: "Use for emphasis within sentences (e.g., '*code is primary*'), book/article titles (e.g., *Code Complete*), technical terms in prose context (e.g., 'the *intent* artifact'), and quoted speech/dialogue (e.g., '*Build X.*')"
      code: "Use for code snippets (`variable_name`), file paths (`intent.yaml`), commands (`ivd validate`), and technical identifiers"
    
    quotations:
      inline_quotes: "Use double quotes for short phrases, dialogue, and examples (e.g., 'The PM said: \"Add export to CSV.\"')"
      block_quotes: "Use '> ' for longer quotes or emphasized statements (sparingly; prefer bold for emphasis)"
      quotable_lines: "Format as bold in prose; repeat in Key Points (e.g., **We have been building at the speed of misunderstanding.**)"
    
    structure:
      paragraph_length: "3-6 sentences typical; 1-2 sentences for emphasis; 7+ for dense exposition (break with subheadings if > 10 sentences)"
      sentence_variety: "Mix short (impact) and long (explanation); avoid 3+ consecutive long sentences"
      lists: "Use bulleted lists for 3+ related items; use '—' (em dash) for inline lists of 2 items"
    
    headings:
      chapter_title: "# Chapter N: Title"
      section: "## N.M Section Title"
      subsection: "### Subsection (rare; prefer flat structure)"
      key_points: "## Key Points (always at chapter end)"
    
    special_elements:
      footnotes: "[^N] inline reference; definitions at section end with blank line between each"
      diagrams: "[Placeholder for diagram] with alt text; actual diagram in assets/"
      code_blocks: "```language (e.g., ```yaml, ```python); always include language tag"
      examples: "Embed in prose with italic framing for dialogue (e.g., '*Build X.*') or quotes for examples (e.g., '\"Add export to CSV\"')"
    
    forbidden:
      - "No ALL CAPS for emphasis (use bold)"
      - "No excessive exclamation marks (!!!)"
      - "No emoji (unless user explicitly requests)"
      - "No underscores for emphasis (markdown renders as italic; use *italic* or **bold** explicitly)"

      - "All experiments run and data collected"
      - "Code repositor

p process for writing each chapter: verify prerequisites, create folder, create intent, write manuscript, cross-check, update chapter status, update book status."
  validated_by: "Chapters 1 and 2 (both complete)"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASES
# ==============================================================================

phases:
  phase_1_content_creation:
    duration: "3-4 months"
    deliverables:
      - "All chapters written (first draft)"
      - "All projects built and tested"
      - "All experiments run and data collected"
      - "Code repository created with examples"
    validation:
      - "Technical review by 3+ experts"
      - "Beta reader feedback (10+ readers)"
      - "All code examples executable"
      - "All data validated and reproducible"
  
  phase_2_validation:
    duration: "1-2 months"
    deliverables:
      - "All claims validated with experiments"
      - "Statistical analysis complete"
      - "Case studies written"
      - "Peer review complete"
    validation:
      - "Each claim has >= 3 validation experiments"
      - "Results are reproducible by independent parties"
      - "Confidence intervals and statistical significance calculated"
  
  phase_3_production:
    duration: "1 month"
    deliverables:
      - "Professional editing complete"
      - "Cover design finished"
      - "EPUB and PDF formatted for Kindle"
      - "Code repository polished"
      - "Landing page and marketing materials"
    validation:
      - "Kindle formatting validated"
      - "All links and references checked"
      - "Beta reader final approval"
  
  phase_4_launch:
    duration: "1 month"
    deliverables:
      - "Amazon Kindle published"
      - "Launch campaign executed"
      - "Initial reviews collected"
      - "Community engagement started"
    validation:
      - "50+ reviews in first month"
      - "Top 10 in AI category"
      - "1,000+ copies sold"
  
  phase_5_growth:
    duration: "ongoing"
    deliverables:
      - "Second edition planning (based on feedback)"
      - "Speaking engagements and workshops"
      - "Community contributions integrated"
      - "Updated recipes and patterns"
    validation:
      - "#1 ranking achieved and maintained"
      - "10,000+ copies sold"
      - "Industry recognition"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE: Why this structure?
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Four-part, 20-chapter, validation-led structure with Amazon success focus"
  
  reasoning: |
    1. Problem → Paradigm → Practice → Proof → Adoption
       - Part I establishes the alignment crisis and IVD as paradigm shift (theory-first; purpose per part)
       - Part II builds real projects (learn by doing); spiral: projects revisit Part I concepts
       - Part III proves claims with data (credibility)
       - Part IV enables team adoption
       - Readers can stop at any layer and have value
    
    2. Validation-led credibility (pattern: Code Complete)
       - Every IVD claim must be backed by experiments

nale:
  decision: "Four-part, 20-chapter, validation-led structure with Amazon success focus"
  
  reasoning: |
    1. Problem → Paradigm → Practice → Proof → Adoption
       - Part I establishes the alignment crisis and IVD as paradigm shift (theory-first; purpose per part)
       - Part II builds real projects (learn by doing); spiral: projects revisit Part I concepts
       - Part III proves claims with data (credibility)
       - Part IV enables team adoption
       - Readers can stop at any layer and have value
    
    2. Validation-led credibility (pattern: Code Complete)
       - Every IVD claim must be backed by experiments (Part III)
       - Code examples and validation-experiments/ are first-class
       - Evidence-based positioning differentiates from opinion-only books
    
    3. Market-driven (Amazon, adoption)
       - Structure and metrics target category rank, reviews, sales
       - Appendices (recipes, validation methodology, tools) support practitioners and dip-in reference
       - Rationale and alternatives from prior ROADMAP preserved in merged intent
    
    4. First-mover in a wave (pattern: Design Patterns, K&R, Code Complete)
       - IVD names and codifies intent-first, AI-aligned development at the right moment in the AI-assisted dev wave
    
    5. Timeless principles + timely tooling (pattern: bestsellers)
       - Emphasize timeless: intent, verification, alignment. Timely: Cursor, Cline, MCP, AI agents. Principles outlast tools.
  
  evidence: "../ivd_system_intent.yaml (principles, verification); ../research/validation_methodology.md; ../research/best_selling_programming_books.md"
  date: "2026-01-25"
  stakeholder: "IVD Framework Maintainer"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES: Why NOT other approaches?
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Paradigm-first 7–11 chapter (prior ROADMAP)"
    rejected_because: |
      - Fewer chapters, less room for validation and proof
      - No dedicated Part III for experiments; weaker evidence-based pitch
      - Appendices-heavy; core narrative lighter on practice and adoption
    when_it_works: "Shorter, theory-focused books; IVD needs proof and practice"
  
  - name: "Theory-only, no projects"
    rejected_because: "IVD must be immediately actionable; principles need implementation and validation"
    when_it_works: "Academic or conceptual treatments only"
  
  - name: "Tutorial-first (start with first artifact)"
    rejected_because: "Readers need paradigm and problem context before HOW; otherwise feels like 'just another tool'"
    when_it_works: "Tools with established paradigms"

# SOURCE MATERIAL: Core references for writers
# ==============================================================================

source_material:
  - "../purpose.md"
  - "../ivd_system_intent.yaml"
  - "../framework.md"
  - "cookbook.md"
  - "cheatsheet.md"
  - "recipe-spec.md"
  - "templates/intent.yaml"
  - "templates/intent_levels_guide.md"
  - "templates/task_level_quick_ref.m

    when_it_works: "Tools with established paradigms"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  evidence_based:
    requirement: "Every claim must be validated with data from real projects"
    rationale: "Credibility comes from proof, not promises"
    validation: "All experiments documented with reproducible methodology"
  
  immediately_actionable:
    requirement: "Readers can apply IVD within 24 hours of reading Part I"
    rationale: "Value comes from doing, not just understanding"
    validation: "Beta readers successfully create first intent artifact same day"
  
  market_driven:
    requirement: "Book structure and content optimized for Amazon success"
    rationale: "Top-selling requires understanding what readers want and how Amazon ranks"
    validation: "Title, subtitle, keywords, and description A/B tested pre-launch"
  
  code_quality:
    requirement: "All code examples are production-ready, tested, and documented"
    rationale: "Readers should copy-paste and ship, not debug"
    validation: "All code in repository with passing tests and CI/CD"
  
  visual_communication:
    requirement: "Complex concepts have diagrams, tables, and visual aids"
    rationale: "Understanding is faster with visual + text"
    validation: "20+ diagrams, all chapters have visual elements"
  
  accessibility:
    requirement: "Written for developers with 2+ years experience, no PhD required"
    rationale: "Broad adoption requires accessibility"
    validation: "Beta readers from diverse backgrounds understand content"
  
  no_timeline_references:
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in book content (days, weeks, months)"
    rationale: "Aligns with ivd_system_intent and workspace rules"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' manuscript/**/*.md | grep -v 'test:' | wc -l == 0"
  
  source_material_accuracy:
    requirement: "All source_material and referenced paths must exist in IVD package

ements"
  
  accessibility:
    requirement: "Written for developers with 2+ years experience, no PhD required"
    rationale: "Broad adoption requires accessibility"
    validation: "Beta readers from diverse backgrounds understand content"
  
  no_timeline_references:
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in book content (days, weeks, months)"
    rationale: "Aligns with ivd_system_intent and workspace rules"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' manuscript/**/*.md | grep -v 'test:' | wc -l == 0"
  
  source_material_accuracy:
    requirement: "All source_material and referenced paths must exist in IVD package"
    rationale: "Book must not cite missing files"
    validation: "Verify all source_material and in-text paths exist"
  
  kindle_compliance:
    requirement: "EPUB output must meet Amazon Kindle requirements"
    rationale: "Publishing target"
    validation: "epubcheck build/*.epub passes"
  
  master_intent_alignment:
    requirement: "Book content must not contradict ../ivd_system_intent.yaml"
    rationale: "Canon consistency"
    validation: "Manual review: book aligns with master intent"
  
  chapter_takeaways:
    requirement: "Each chapter ends with a 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block; where applicable, include a 'Checklist' (e.g. verification, adoption, pre-flight)"
    rationale: "Scannability and application; pattern: Code Complete. Readers who skim still get takeaways."
    validation: "Manual or grep: each chapter file contains 'Key Points' or 'Summary' section; checklists in Ch4, 16–20, Appendix A"
  
  quotable_principles:
    requirement: "The 7 IVD principles and core ideas (e.g. 'Intent as contract') must be stateable as short, memorable, tweetable rules. See writing_style.quotable_lines and principles_as_rules."
    rationale: "Memorable, citable, shareable; pattern: Clean Code, Pragmatic Programmer"
    validation: "Manual: each principle has a one-line rule in Ch3 and in Appendix A; 1–2 quotable lines per chapter where natural"
  
  author_sourced_examples:
    requirement: |
      Case studies and narratives that illustrate real-world failure or success must derive from the
      author's direct experience (anonymized as needed), or be explicitly labeled as industry composite
      or third-party when the author was not directly involved. The book is anchored by the author's
      own journey (Preface) and, where possible, the author's own examples. No invented, unanchored
      fiction. Composite is the exception, not the default.
    rationale: "Author credibility; research (best_selling_programming_books.md 6.1): Fowler, McConnell, GoF, Hunt & Thomas use examples from their practice, consulting, or design work. Composites are not the pattern."
    validation: "Manual: each case study or detailed failure/success narrative has attribution (author's experience, or 'composite'/'industry' with explicit label). Preface contains author's journey."

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  success_criteria:
    - metric: "Amazon ranking"
      ta

ty; research (best_selling_programming_books.md 6.1): Fowler, McConnell, GoF, Hunt & Thomas use examples from their practice, consulting, or design work. Composites are not the pattern."
    validation: "Manual: each case study or detailed failure/success narrative has attribution (author's experience, or 'composite'/'industry' with explicit label). Preface contains author's journey."

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  success_criteria:
    - metric: "Amazon ranking"
      target: "#1 in AI & Machine Learning category"
      measurement: "Amazon Best Sellers Rank"
    
    - metric: "Sales volume"
      target: "10,000+ copies in year 1"
      measurement: "Amazon KDP sales dashboard"
    
    - metric: "Reader satisfaction"
      target: "4.5+ star average with 1,000+ reviews"
      measurement: "Amazon review aggregation"
    
    - metric: "Technical validation"
      target: "All claims proven with data"
      measurement: "Validation experiments in Part III"
    
    - metric: "Community adoption"
      target: "1,000+ teams adopt IVD"
      measurement: "Community survey and GitHub stars"
    
    - metric: "Industry recognition"
      target: "Featured in 10+ publications/podcasts"
      measurement: "Media tracking"
  
  test_path: "validation_experiments/*.py"
  evidence: "experiment_results/*.md"

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS AND MITIGATION
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  technical_risk:
    risk: "Validation experiments don't prove claims"
    probability: "low"
    impact: "high"
    mitigation: "Run experiments early, adjust claims if needed, conservative estimates"
  
  market_risk:
    risk: "Amazon algorithm changes, category competition increases"
    probability: "medium"
    impact: "medium"
    mitigation: "Focus on quality and reviews, organic marketing, community building"
  
  timing_risk:
    risk: "AI development landscape shifts before publication"
    probability: "medium"
    impact: "medium"
    mitigation: "Focus on timeless principles, quick publication timeline, update strategy"
  
  adoption_risk:
    risk: "IVD is too complex for average developer"
    probability: "low"
    impact: "high"
    mitigation: "Simple start (Part I in 2 hours), progressive complexity, strong examples"

    - "Build Project 1 (Chapter 5) in parallel to validate approach"
    - "Run first validation experiment (context efficiency)"
  
  tools:
    - "Markdown for writing (portable, versionable)"
    - "Pandoc for EPUB/PDF generation"
    - "GitHub for version control and collaboration"
    - "Amazon KDP for publishing"
    - "Python for validation experiments"
  
  repository_structure: |
    ivd-book/

    │   │   └── ...

changelog:
  - version: "1.3"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    changes: |
      author_sourced_examples: Case studies and failure/success narratives must derive from the
      author's direct experience (anonymized) or be explicitly labeled as composite/third-party.
      Rationale: research 6.1—Fowler, McConnell, GoF, Hunt & Thomas use examples from their
      practice/consulting/design work; composites are not the pattern. Chapter intents (e.g. Ch1)
      and research doc 6.1 updated.
  - version: "1.2"
    date: "2026-01-25"

      quotable_lines, principles_as_rules). Enh
